candidate_paths.name: Pusher Channels Android Client Quick Start
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative platform-specific client onboarding path for Android apps instead of JavaScript web client.
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Official Android quick start guide provides a native mobile onboarding path with similar steps adapted for Android environment.

steps.action: Create a Pusher account and Channels app to get API keys.
details: Copy app_id, key, secret, and cluster from App Keys.
steps.failure_or_wait: Account creation or app creation errors or delays.
steps.input: Account registration data and app creation inputs.
steps.success_signal: API keys are available in dashboard.

steps.action: Add Pusher Java client library dependency via Gradle or Maven.
steps.output: Pusher Java client library available in project.

steps.action: Create a Pusher connection with key and cluster and connect.
details: Instantiate Pusher with key and options, call connect().

steps.failure_or_wait: Subscription errors or invalid channel name.

steps.action: Bind an event listener to an event name.
details: Use channel.bind('my-event', SubscriptionEventListener) to listen for events.
steps.failure_or_wait: Binding errors or event not received.
steps.input: Event name string and listener implementation.
steps.success_signal: Listener callback executes when event received.

steps.action: Trigger an event from the server using server SDK with app credentials.
details: Use server library to trigger 'my-event' on 'my-channel'.
steps.input: API keys, channel name, event name, and event data.

steps.action: Observe the event listener executing in the Android app upon receiving the triggered event.
details: Listener callback runs, e.g., logs event data.
steps.failure_or_wait: No event received or listener errors.
steps.success_signal: Visible or logged event data in client app.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: If you published an event and it triggered your alert(...) call, well done!

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer successfully receives and handles a real-time event in their web application using Pusher Channels.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The bound event handler function executes in the web app, e.g., an alert or console log triggered by the received event.
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Receiving a triggered event in the web app after subscribing and binding to a channel and event.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The documentation explicitly states that triggering an event and having it received and handled in the client app is the first success milestone.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has a web application environment ready to integrate JavaScript.
Developer has basic knowledge of JavaScript and web development.
Developer has internet access to load external scripts.
entry_point.developer_intent: Send and receive real-time events in a web application using Pusher Channels.

friction_gates.description: Developer must create a Pusher account and Channels app to obtain API keys.
Developer must include the Pusher client library in their app (JavaScript or Android).
Server SDK requires valid app credentials (app_id, key, secret, cluster) to trigger events.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a free Pusher account and create a Channels app to obtain API keys (app_id, key, secret, cluster).
Have a web application environment capable of including JavaScript and running client-side code.

primary_path.action: Create a Pusher account and Channels app to get API keys.
details: Copy app_id, key, secret, and cluster from App Keys.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account creation or app creation errors or delays.
primary_path.input: Account registration data and app creation inputs.
primary_path.success_signal: API keys are available in dashboard.

primary_path.action: Include the Pusher JavaScript client library in the web app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Script loading errors or network issues.
primary_path.input: Web app HTML or template files.
primary_path.output: Pusher JS client library loaded in the web app.
primary_path.success_signal: Pusher object available in JavaScript runtime.

primary_path.action: Open a connection to Pusher Channels using the key and cluster.
details: Instantiate Pusher with key and cluster parameters.

primary_path.failure_or_wait: Subscription errors or invalid channel name.

primary_path.action: Bind an event handler function to an event name (e.g., 'my-event').
details: Use channel.bind('my-event', callback) to listen for events.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Binding errors or event not received.
primary_path.input: Event name string and callback function.

primary_path.action: Trigger an event from the server using server SDK with app credentials.
details: Use server library (e.g., Node.js, Ruby, PHP) to trigger 'my-event' on 'my-channel'.
primary_path.input: API keys, channel name, event name, and event data.

primary_path.action: Observe the event handler executing in the web app upon receiving the triggered event.
details: Event handler callback runs, e.g., alert or console log.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No event received or handler errors.
primary_path.success_signal: Visible or logged event data in client app.

sections_used: Listen for events on your channel
sources.title: Pusher Channels Docs - JavaScript Quick Start
Pusher Channels Docs - Android Quick Start Guide
Getting started with Pusher Beams | Pusher tutorials

alternatives_considered.name: Pusher Channels Android Client
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Android quick start is platform-specific and less universal than the JavaScript client which runs in browsers and is the first documented example.

alternatives_considered.name: Pusher Beams Push Notifications
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Focused on push notifications setup and requires additional platform-specific setup (e.g., Apple developer account), thus more complex and not the primary first-mile onboarding for Channels.

surface.name: Pusher Channels JavaScript Client
surface.selection_basis: The JavaScript quick start guide is the primary documented onboarding surface for developers to achieve first success with Pusher Channels, as it is prominently featured in official docs and includes detailed step-by-step instructions.

uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor-claimed time, we cannot report a precise time-to-first-success metric.
uncertainties.question: Is there an explicitly documented time-to-first-success claim by Pusher for Channels or Beams?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit timing or duration claims found in official documentation or tutorials.

uncertainties.impact: Different products may have different onboarding flows and first success definitions.
uncertainties.question: Is the first success milestone the same for Pusher Beams as for Channels?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Pusher Beams documentation focuses on instance creation and notification sending but does not clearly define a first success milestone comparable to Channels.
